text:
Once upon a cool spring afternoon, Mr. Pink Feather wished for wings. He fluffed his feathers and imagined how wonderful it would be to soar above the fields; to feel the soft grass whisking past. He practiced fluttering his wings.

His friend Mr. Lineguard thought it was quite peculiar.

"Ever thought of actually getting some wings?" Mr. Lineguard asked one morning.

"It wouldn’t just fly away," Mr. Feather countered.

"The idea?"

"You'd get dizzy easy," laughed Mr. Feather.

"And waste good feathers."

"Okay," conceded Mr. Feather thoughtfully.

He spent his days collecting feathers.

Years passed, and Mr. Feather grew older.

One day, he lifted his wings.

With a gentle flap...

...and off he went!

His first flight was gentle, but gradually, he learned to glide.

Over the hills, he saw a pod of whales.

He swooped down...

...and caught some seaweed.

Back home—
